revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23972795306|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Britse Eilande]][[als:Britische Inseln]][[an:Islas britanicas]][[ast:Islles britániques]][[az:Britaniya adaları]][[be_x_old:Брытанскія астравы]][[be:Брытанскія астравы]][[bg:Британски острови]][[bn:ব্রিটিশ দ্বীপপুঞ্জ]][[bs:Britanska ostrva]][[ca:Illes Britàniques]][[ceb:British Isles]][[cs:Britské ostrovy]][[cy:Ynysoedd Prydain]][[diq:Adey Britanya]][[el:Βρετανικά Νησιά]][[eo:Britaj Insuloj]][[et:Briti saared]][[fa:جزایر بریتانیا]][[fi:Britteinsaaret]][[frp:Iles britaniques]][[frr:Britisk Eilunen]][[fy:Britske Eilannen]][[ga:Éire agus an Bhreatain Mhór]][[gl:Illas británicas]][[gv:Ny h-Ellanyn Goaldagh]][[he:האיים הבריטיים]][[hif:British Isles]][[hi:ब्रिटिश आइल्स]][[hr:Britanski otoci]][[hsb:Britiske kupy]][[hy:Բրիտանական կղզիներ]][[ia:Insulas Britannic]][[id:Kepulauan Britania]][[is:Bretlandseyjar]][[ka:ბრიტანეთის კუნძულები]][[kk:Британ аралдары]][[kw:Enesow Keltek]][[ky:Британ аралдары]][[lb:Brittesch Inselen]][[lmo:Isole Britanege]][[lo:ເກາະອັງກິດ]][[lt:Britų salos]][[lv:Britu salas]][[min:Kapulauan Baritania]][[mk:Британски Острови]][[mr:ब्रिटिश बेटे]][[ms:Kepulauan British]][[mwl:Ilhas Británicas]][[nl:Britse Eilanden]][[nn:Dei britiske øyane]][[no:De britiske øyer]][[nrm:Îles Britanniques]][[oc:Illas Britanicas]][[pl:Wyspy Brytyjskie]][[pms:Ìsole britàniche]][[pnb:جزائر برطانیہ]][[pt:Ilhas Britânicas]][[rmy:Britanikane dvipa]][[ro:Insulele Britanice]][[scn:Ìsuli britànnichi]][[sco:Breetish Isles]][[sc:Ìsulas Britànnicas]][[sh:Britanski otoci]][[si:බ්‍රිතාන්‍ය දිවයින්]][[sk:Britské ostrovy]][[sl:Britansko otočje]][[sr:Британска острва]][[stq:Britiske Ailounde]][[sv:Brittiska öarna]][[sw:Funguvisiwa la Britania]][[ta:பிரித்தானியத் தீவுகள்]][[th:บริติชไอลส์]][[tl:Kapuluang Britaniko]][[tr:Britanya Adaları]][[tt:Британия утраулары]][[uk:Британські острови]][[ur:جزائر برطانیہ]][[uz:Britaniya orollari]][[vi:Quần đảo Anh]][[war:Kapuropud-an Britaniko]][[wuu:不列颠群岛]][[xmf:ბრიტანეთიშ კოკეფი]][[yi:בריטישע אינזלען]][[zh-min-nan:Tāi-eng Kûn-tó]][[zh-yue:不列顛群島]] [[ar:الجزر البريطانية]] [[da:De Britiske Øer]] [[de:Britische Inseln]] [[en:British Isles]] [[es:Islas Británicas]] [[eu:Britainiar uharteak]] [[fr:Îles Britanniques]] [[hu:Brit-szigetek]] [[it:Isole britanniche]] [[ja:ブリテン諸島]] [[ko:브리튼 제도]] [[la:Insulae Britannicae]] [[ru:Британские острова]] [[simple:British Isles]] [[zh:不列顛群島]]
[[en:British Isles]]
{{wikidata link|Q38272}} There are no official, public Shinto shrines (''jinja'') in the United Kingdom.<ref name="greenshinto2011">{{cite web |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2011/07/14/project-for-a-uk-jinja/ |title=Project for a UK Jinja |website=Green Shinto |date=14 July 2011 |access-date=February 18, 2026}}</ref> However, several notable Shinto shrine structures exist within Japanese gardens or as historical features.
The [[Tatton Park]] estate in [[Cheshire]] features a historic Japanese garden constructed in 1910, inspired by the Anglo-Japanese Exhibition. This garden includes a Shinto shrine and other artefacts reputedly imported from Japan specifically for the garden's creation. It is considered one of the finest examples of a Japanese garden in Europe.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.tattonpark.org.uk/what_to_see_and_do/gardens/garden_areas/japanese_garden.aspx |title=Japanese Garden |website=Tatton Park |access-date=February 18, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tatton_Park_Gardens |title=Tatton Park Gardens |website=Wikipedia |access-date=February 18, 2026}}</ref>
In [[Hertfordshire]], a small Shinto shrine (specifically an ''Azumi-Inari'' or fox shrine sacred to the God of Prosperity) is located in the Japanese garden at The Garden House, Cottered. This timber structure, carved and stained black, was made in Japan for Herbert Goode (who began the garden in 1905) and dates to the early 20th century or earlier. It is a [[Grade II listed building]] (List Entry Number 1348015).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1348015 |title=Shinto Shrine in Japanese Garden at Garden House 20 Meters to West-south-west of House, Church Lane |website=Historic England |access-date=February 18, 2026}}</ref>
Other related sites and initiatives include:
* [[Japan House London]], which hosts cultural events and exhibitions related to Shinto, such as ''[[kagura]]'' performances and talks involving Shinto priests.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.japanhouselondon.uk/whats-on/kagura-and-shinto-performance-talk-by-kasuga-taisha-and-niukawakami-shrine-with-chief-priests-kasannoin-hirotada-kusaka-yasuhiro/ |title=Kagura and Shinto Performance Talk |website=Japan House London |access-date=February 18, 2026}}</ref> * [[Three Wheels Temple]] in London, a [[Jōdo Shinshū]] (Shin Buddhist) temple rather than a Shinto shrine, but an important centre for Japanese spirituality in the UK. * Past proposals to establish a formal Shinto shrine, including initiatives discussed on the Green Shinto blog to create a UK ''jinja'' or small ''[[hokora]]'' to serve the local community.<ref name="greenshinto2011" />
As of the 2010s, efforts to establish a dedicated public Shinto shrine in the UK (such as the "UK Jinja" project near Bath) had been proposed but not realized as an official, operational public shrine.<ref name="greenshinto2011" />
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.tattonpark.org.uk/what_to_see_and_do/gardens/garden_areas/japanese_garden.aspx Japanese Garden at Tatton Park] * [https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1348015 Historic England listing for the Hertfordshire shrine] * [https://www.greenshinto.com/ Green Shinto blog] (discussions on Shinto in the UK)[[Category:Islands of the Republic of Ireland]] [[Category:Islands of the United Kingdom]] [[Category:British Isles]] [[Category:Regions of Europe]] [[Category:Islands of Europe]] [[Category:Geographical naming disputes]]
